info.title: Ziona API
info.description:
Authentication REST API for Ziona mobile app.

**GraphQL endpoint:** `/graphql/` (use GraphiQL for queries and mutations)

**Auth Flow:** Register → Verify Email (OTP) → Login → Use Bearer token → Refresh → Logout

**Response Format:** All responses format JSON keys in camelCase. Success responses are wrapped in `{success: true, data: {...}}`. Errors use `{success: false, error: {code, message, details}}`.

post.description: Creates a new user account with username and date of birth, or updates an existing unverified user. Sends a 6-digit OTP to verify email. Does NOT return tokens.

201.description: New user registered successfully. OTP sent.

message.example: Registration successful. Please verify your email with the OTP sent.

200.description: Existing unverified user updated. New OTP sent.

message.example: Account updated. Please verify your email with the new OTP sent.

post.description: Authenticates user. If verified, returns tokens. If unverified, sends an OTP and returns requiresVerification=true.

200.description: Login successful. Structure differs based on verification status.

tokens.description: Present ONLY if user is verified

requiresVerification.description: Present ONLY if user is unverified

message.description: Present ONLY if user is unverified, indicating OTP sent.

post.description: Public endpoint to check if an email exists before attempting login. Used to trigger UX flows like 'Sign up' vs 'Enter Password'. Rate limited heavily (10 requests per minute).
Exchange a valid refresh token for a new token pair.
Blacklists access token and revokes refresh token.

get.description: Retrieves the authenticated user's full profile and engagement statistics.

delete.description: Schedules reversible account deletion after a 30-day grace period. Existing sessions are revoked immediately and the account is hidden. Provide password or otp/code plus acknowledgePermanentDeletion=true. For compatibility, acknowledgePermanentDeletion may also be sent as a DELETE query parameter.

post.description: Change the authenticated user password. Supports optionally signing out other devices.
Send a one-time code for registration, verification, password reset, or account actions.
Verify a previously sent OTP. Response shape varies by purpose.
Deactivate the authenticated account using password or OTP re-authentication.
Schedule reversible account deletion after a 30-day grace period using password or OTP re-authentication. Requires acknowledgePermanentDeletion=true.
Explicitly reactivate an account using the short-lived recovery token returned by a valid login. No normal session is issued before confirmation.

200.description: Account reactivated and fresh tokens issued

401.description: Recovery token is invalid or expired

post.description: Cancel a pending deletion during the grace period using the short-lived recovery token returned by a valid login.

200.description: Deletion cancelled and fresh tokens issued

401.description: Recovery token is invalid or expired

post.description: Verifies user's email using a 6-digit OTP code. Returns tokens immediately on success.

400.description: Invalid OTP (includes attemptsRemaining in details) or OTP Expired

429.description: Too many verification attempts (MAX_ATTEMPTS_REACHED includes retryAfter)

post.description: Resends a new 6-digit OTP to the user's email.

200.description: OTP resent (if account exists and is unverified)

message.example: If an account with this email exists and is unverified, a new code has been sent.

429.description: Too many resend requests (RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED includes retryAfter in details)

post.description: Generates 4 unique, available username suggestions based on email and date of birth.
Sends a 6-digit OTP to the user's email. Always returns success.

message.example: If an account with this email exists, a reset code has been sent.

post.description: Resets the user's password using the OTP sent to their email.
Allows Google OAuth users to select their permanent username. Changes the temporary username generating during login and clears the needsUsernameSelection flag.
Authenticates or registers a user using a Google ID token from Firebase.

id_token.description: Google ID token from Firebase Auth

post.description: Issues a short-lived server nonce challenge for Sign in with Apple. The mobile app should send the returned `nonce` to Apple and send the returned `rawNonce` back to `/auth/apple` with the Apple identity token.

rawNonce.description: Plain nonce generated by the backend. Send this back to `/auth/apple` as `rawNonce`.

nonce.description: SHA-256 hashed nonce. Send this to Apple's authorization request.

post.description: Authenticates or registers a user using Apple's identity token. Apple only sends user profile data such as name and email on the first authorization, so subsequent logins may omit the `user` object.

identityToken.description: Apple identity token returned by Sign in with Apple.

user.description: Optional Apple user payload. Usually available only on first Apple authorization.

400.description: Missing fields, invalid nonce, or Apple did not provide an email for first sign-in

401.description: Invalid, expired, or untrusted Apple identity token

503.description: Apple public keys could not be fetched

BearerAuth.description: JWT access token from verify-email or login

needsUsernameSelection.description: True if an OAuth user needs to select a username

details.description: Optional metadata like attemptsRemaining or retryAfter

signOutOtherDevices.description: When true, revoke all other refresh/access sessions except the current token.

DeleteAccountRequest.description: Provide either password or otp/code, plus acknowledgePermanentDeletion=true.

password.description: Current account password. Required when deleting with password re-authentication.

code.description: Alias for otp. Supported for compatibility.

acknowledgePermanentDeletion.description: Must be true to confirm permanent account deletion.

code.description: Alias for otp. Supported for compatibility.

data.description: Shape varies by purpose. Account deletion/deactivation returns { verified, purpose }.
